From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

1982 film by Bruno Corbucci

| La casa stregata | |
| --- | --- |
| Theatrical release poster by [Renato Casaro](/wiki/Renato_Casaro "Renato Casaro")[[1]](#cite_note-1) | |

| Screenplay by | * [Mario Amendola](/wiki/Mario_Amendola "Mario Amendola") * Bruno Corbucci * [Enrico Oldoini](/wiki/Enrico_Oldoini "Enrico Oldoini")[[2]](#cite_note-FOOTNOTECurti201999-2)[[3]](#cite_note-FOOTNOTECurti2019100-3) |
| Produced by | * [Mario Cecchi Gori](/wiki/Mario_Cecchi_Gori "Mario Cecchi Gori") * [Vittorio Cecchi Gori](/wiki/Vittorio_Cecchi_Gori "Vittorio Cecchi Gori")[[3]](#cite_note-FOOTNOTECurti2019100-3) |

| Music by | [Detto Mariano](/wiki/Detto_Mariano "Detto Mariano")[[3]](#cite_note-FOOTNOTECurti2019100-3) |

| Release date | * 4 March 1982 (1982-03-04) (Italy) |

| Country | Italy[[3]](#cite_note-FOOTNOTECurti2019100-3) |

***La casa stregata*** (transl. The Haunted House[[2]](#cite_note-FOOTNOTECurti201999-2)) is a 1982 Italian [comedy film](/wiki/Comedy_film "Comedy film") directed by [Bruno Corbucci](/wiki/Bruno_Corbucci "Bruno Corbucci") and starring [Renato Pozzetto](/wiki/Renato_Pozzetto "Renato Pozzetto") and [Gloria Guida](/wiki/Gloria_Guida "Gloria Guida").[[2]](#cite_note-FOOTNOTECurti201999-2)[[3]](#cite_note-FOOTNOTECurti2019100-3)

## Plot

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=La_casa_stregata&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Plot")]

In a mythical "Arabian Nights" prologue two crossed lovers are struck by a curse cast unto them by a powerful witch ([Lia Zoppelli](/wiki/Lia_Zoppelli "Lia Zoppelli")) who had betrothed her own daughter, Candida, ([Gloria Guida](/wiki/Gloria_Guida "Gloria Guida")) to the evil tyrant Ali Amman, while the girl was passionately in love with "Giorgiafat" ([Renato Pozzetto](/wiki/Renato_Pozzetto "Renato Pozzetto")); the witch turns the two lovers into salt statues forcing their disembodied souls to wander for a thousand years before re-incarnating and meeting again.

If they would be able to prolong Candida's virginity until the thousandth anniversary of the curse expires, they will be able to live happily, if not the curse would renew itself.

A thousand years later Giorgio is a young Italian bank clerk who has to move from Milan to Rome for work reasons, promising his girlfriend Candida to find a suitable abode for them and her own mother (again, [Lia Zoppelli](/wiki/Lia_Zoppelli "Lia Zoppelli")). After many comical incidents Giorgio finds a luxurious mansion for rent at a ludicrously low price and is soon joined by Candida and her mother.

The couple is soon married, but a series of supernatural incidents prevents them from consummating; the poltergeist activity seems centered around Giorgio's pet dog (a Great Dane who starts talking in a heavy southern accent) and a mysterious character who looks like the faithful servant of "Giorgiafat".

## Cast

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=La_casa_stregata&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Cast")]

* [Renato Pozzetto](/wiki/Renato_Pozzetto "Renato Pozzetto") as Giorgio Allegri
* [Gloria Guida](/wiki/Gloria_Guida "Gloria Guida") as Candida Melengo
* [Yorgo Voyagis](/wiki/Yorgo_Voyagis "Yorgo Voyagis") as Oscar
* [Lia Zoppelli](/wiki/Lia_Zoppelli "Lia Zoppelli") as Anastasia, Candida's Mother
* Angelo Pellegrino as Elpidio
* Leo Gavero as The Bank Manager
* [Franco Diogene](/wiki/Franco_Diogene "Franco Diogene") as The Realtor
* Vittorio Ripamonti as Don Alvino
* Nicola Morelli as Albani
* [Bruno Corbucci](/wiki/Bruno_Corbucci "Bruno Corbucci") as The Vet (uncredited)[[3]](#cite_note-FOOTNOTECurti2019100-3)
* Marilda Donà as Lucia

## Production

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=La_casa_stregata&action=edit&section=3 "Edit section: Production")]

*La casa stregata* was shot on location in [Milan](/wiki/Milan "Milan"), [Rome](/wiki/Rome "Rome") and at Villa Giovanelli-Fogaccia and DePaolis In.Ci.R. Studios.[[3]](#cite_note-FOOTNOTECurti2019100-3) Among the cast was [Gloria Guida](/wiki/Gloria_Guida "Gloria Guida"), in which this was among her last films he appeared in.[[3]](#cite_note-FOOTNOTECurti2019100-3)

## Release

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=La_casa_stregata&action=edit&section=4 "Edit section: Release")]

*La casa stregata* was distributed theatrically by [Cineriz](/wiki/Cineriz "Cineriz") on March 4, 1982 in Italy.[[3]](#cite_note-FOOTNOTECurti2019100-3)

## Reception

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=La_casa_stregata&action=edit&section=5 "Edit section: Reception")]

From contemporary reviews, Aldo Vigano of *[La Stampa](/wiki/La_Stampa "La Stampa")* criticized Corbucci's direction and found the film to be similar to [Giorgio Capitani](/wiki/Giorgio_Capitani "Giorgio Capitani")'s film *[Bollenti spiriti](/wiki/Bollenti_spiriti "Bollenti spiriti")* released the previous year.[[4]](#cite_note-FOOTNOTECurti2019101-4)[[5]](#cite_note-5) Leonardo Autera of *[Corriere della Sera](/wiki/Corriere_della_Sera "Corriere della Sera")* complained about the profanity, finding it "repetitive to the point of boredom."[[4]](#cite_note-FOOTNOTECurti2019101-4)[[6]](#cite_note-6)

## See also

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=La_casa_stregata&action=edit&section=6 "Edit section: See also")]

* [List of Italian films of 1982](/wiki/List_of_Italian_films_of_1982 "List of Italian films of 1982")
